notes: Increased from $14900 in 2024; additional $1950 if blind or age 65+
Increased from $29200 in 2024; additional $1550 per qualifying spouse if blind or age 65+
Same as single filer amount
Head of Household filing status

phase_out: Taxable income: Single >$197,300 / MFJ >$394,600 (full phase-out at $247,300 / $494,600)
notes: Section 199A. Specified service trades (law, health, consulting) subject to phase-out. W-2 wage / UBIA limitation applies above threshold.

phase_out: Single w/ employer plan: MAGI $79,000-$89,000; MFJ w/ employer plan: $126,000-$146,000
notes: Catch-up: additional $1,000 if age 50+. Full deduction if no employer plan regardless of income.
Not tax-deductible but tax-free growth. Catch-up: additional $1,000 if age 50+. Backdoor Roth available above limits.
Catch-up: additional $7,500 if age 50+. Super catch-up: $11,250 if ages 60-63 (SECURE 2.0). Total employee + employer limit $70,000.
Self-employed: ~20% of net SE income after SE tax deduction. Employer contribution only; no employee deferral.
Catch-up: additional $3,500 if age 50+. Employer must match up to 3% or contribute 2% non-elective.
Employee deferral ($23,500) + employer profit sharing (25% of comp). Catch-up adds $7,500 if 50+.
Catch-up: additional $1,000 if age 55+. Must have qualifying HDHP. Triple tax advantage.
Catch-up: additional $1,000 if age 55+. HDHP minimum deductible: $1,650 self / $3,300 family.
$10,000 cap ($5,000 MFS). Includes state income/sales tax + property tax. Scheduled to expire after 2025 under TCJA.
Pre-12/15/2017 mortgages: $1M limit grandfathered. Home equity loan interest deductible only if used to buy/build/improve home.
Excess carries forward 5 years. Must have written acknowledgment for gifts >$250. Cash to public charities.
FMV deduction for long-term capital gain property. Qualified appraisal required for non-cash >$5,000.
Excess carries forward 5 years. Enhanced deduction for food inventory donations (15%).
Only amount OVER 7.5% of AGI is deductible. Includes premiums, copays, prescriptions, dental, vision, long-term care.
Above-the-line deduction. Must be K-12 teacher, instructor, counselor, or principal with 900+ hours.
Above-the-line deduction. Cannot be claimed if MFS. Includes interest on qualified education loans.
First 4 years of post-secondary education. 100% of first $2,000 + 25% of next $2,000 of qualified expenses.
No limit on years claimed. Cannot claim both AOTC and LLC for same student in same year.
Refundable up to $1,700 (ACTC). Child must be under 17, have SSN, claimed as dependent.

phase_out: Credit percentage: 35% at $0 AGI down to 20% at $43K+ AGI
notes: Non-refundable credit. Must have earned income. Expenses for care of child <13 or disabled dependent.

deduction: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) - No Children
notes: Fully refundable. Must have earned income. Investment income must be <$11,600.
Fully refundable. Qualifying child must meet age, residency, and relationship tests.
Fully refundable.
Fully refundable.

phase_out: Phase-out begins at $3,130,000 of total property placed in service
notes: Allows immediate expensing of qualifying business property. SUVs limited to $31,300. Not available for real property (generally).
TCJA bonus depreciation phases down: 40% in 2025, 20% in 2026, 0% in 2027. Applies to new and used property.
Luxury auto limits. Passenger vehicles >6,000 lbs GVWR exempt from luxury limits (Section 179 SUV cap still applies).
Second year luxury auto limit.
Third year luxury auto limit.
Fourth and subsequent year luxury auto limit until fully depreciated.

applies_to: All (self-employed or employee with employer plan)
notes: Cannot use if claiming actual expenses in prior year for same vehicle. First year choice is binding.
Moving expense deduction only for active-duty military. Medical mileage subject to 7.5% AGI threshold.
Set by statute; does not adjust annually.

limit: $5/sq ft up to 300 sq ft ($1500 max)
notes: Cannot exceed gross income from business. Simplified method: no depreciation recapture on sale.

limit: Actual expenses pro-rated by business % of home
notes: Business percentage = office sq ft / total home sq ft. Includes mortgage interest, taxes, insurance, utilities, repairs, depreciation.
Above-the-line deduction for employer-equivalent portion of SE tax. SE tax rate = 15.3% (12.4% SS + 2.9% Medicare).
Above-the-line. Cannot also claim on Schedule A. Covers self, spouse, dependents, and children under 27.
Repealed by TCJA for tax years after 2017. Replaced by Section 199A QBI deduction for pass-throughs.
NOLs can only offset 80% of taxable income (TCJA). Indefinite carryforward, no carryback (except farming).

phase_out: Exempt if average annual gross receipts <$30M
notes: ATI = taxable income + interest + depreciation + amortization + depletion. Excess carries forward indefinitely.
Meals must be directly related to business. 100% deductible: employer-provided for convenience, company events, marketing events.
Fully non-deductible after TCJA. Tickets, golf, sporting events, etc. Meals can be separated if stated separately.
Incidental costs (wrapping, shipping) excluded from $25 limit. Signs/display items <$4 excluded.

limit: $2,500 per item (no AFS) / $5,000 per item (with AFS)
notes: Allows immediate expensing of tangible property below threshold. Must make annual election. AFS = audited financial statements.

limit: Up to 20% of qualified research expenses over base
notes: Alternative simplified credit: 14% of QRE over 50% of prior 3-year average. Small businesses can offset AMT and payroll tax.
Varies by target group. Must file Form 8850 within 28 days of start date. Long-term family assistance: $9,600.

phase_out: Must hold >5 years; C-Corp with <$50M in assets at issuance
notes: Section 1202. Original issuance stock only. Certain industries excluded (finance, law, engineering, etc.).
Non-refundable but carries forward 5 years. Special needs adoption: full credit regardless of actual expenses.
30% of cost. Includes insulation, windows, doors, heat pumps, biomass stoves. Through 2032.
Solar, wind, geothermal, battery storage (>3 kWh). No annual dollar limit. Through 2034 (steps down after 2032).

phase_out: MAGI: Single $150K / HOH $225K / MFJ $300K; MSRP: $55K cars / $80K trucks-SUVs-vans
notes: Must meet critical mineral and battery component requirements. Transferable to dealer at point of sale.

phase_out: MAGI: Single $75K / HOH $112.5K / MFJ $150K; sale price must be <$25K
notes: Model year must be at least 2 years old. One credit per vehicle. First transfer only.
Portability: unused exemption transfers to surviving spouse. Scheduled to revert to ~$7M (adjusted) after 2025.
$19,000 per recipient per year with no gift tax or reporting. Married couples: $38,000 per recipient (gift splitting).
Must meet bona fide residence or physical presence test (330 days). Housing exclusion available in addition.
